subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle Motion — Velocity, Acceleration & Position
stimulus: A particle moves along the $x$-axis so that its velocity at time $t \ge 0$ is given by $v(t) = \ln(t^2 - 4t + 5) - 0.2t$.

parts.prompt: There is one time, $t = t_R$, in the interval $0 < t < 2$ when the particle is at rest (not moving). Find $t_R$. For $0 < t < t_R$, is the particle moving to the right or to the left? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: Accept $t_R = 1.426$ or $1.425$. The direction point requires stating $v(t) > 0$ for $0 < t < t_R$ as justification. A response that only says 'moving right' without referencing the sign of $v(t)$ does not earn the second point.

scoring_points.description: $t_R \approx 1.426$ (or $1.425$); found by solving $v(t) = 0$ using a calculator

required_elements: $t_R \approx 1.426$ (or $1.425$); found by solving $v(t) = 0$ using a calculator

wrong_examples: does not earn the second point

scoring_points.description: The particle is moving to the right for $0 < t < t_R$ because $v(t) > 0$ on that interval (with explanation referencing sign of $v$)

required_elements: The particle is moving to the right for $0 < t < t_R$ because $v(t) > 0$ on that interval (with explanation referencing sign of $v$)

wrong_examples: does not earn the second point

parts.prompt: Find the acceleration of the particle at time $t = 1.5$. Show the setup for your calculations. Is the speed of the particle increasing or decreasing at time $t = 1.5$? Explain your reasoning.
parts.scoring_notes: The setup point requires showing the derivative expression or the calculator computation clearly. $v(1.5) \approx -0.077$ and $a(1.5) \approx -1$. Speed is increasing when velocity and acceleration have the same sign. A response that incorrectly identifies the sign of $v(1.5)$ or $a(1.5)$ loses the second point.

scoring_points.description: Speed is increasing at $t = 1.5$ because $v(1.5) \approx -0.077 < 0$ and $a(1.5) \approx -1 < 0$ have the same sign

parts.prompt: The position of the particle at time $t$ is $x(t)$, and its position at time $t = 1$ is $x(1) = -3$. Find the position of the particle at time $t = 4$. Show the setup for your calculations.
parts.scoring_notes: Must show the integral setup. $\int_1^4 v(t)\, dt \approx 0.197$. Accept $x(4) \approx -2.803$ or $-2.802$. Incorrect initial condition loses point 2 but may still earn setup and answer points consistently.

wrong_examples: Incorrect initial condition loses point 2 but may still earn setup and answer points consistently
Incorrect initial condition loses point 2 but may still earn setup and answer points consistently
Incorrect initial condition loses point 2 but may still earn setup and answer points consistently

parts.prompt: Find the total distance traveled by the particle over the interval $1 \le t \le 4$. Show the setup for your calculations.
parts.scoring_notes: The setup point is earned for $\int_1^4 |v(t)|\, dt$ or equivalent split integral. Equivalently, splitting at the zeros $t \approx 1.426$ and $t \approx 2.883$ also earns the point. Accept answers of $0.958$, $0.959$, or $0.96$.

scoring_points.description: Correct integral setup using absolute value

required_elements: Correct integral setup using absolute value
